title: Existing register artifact and UART evidence audit
question: Do preserved UART/register comparison artifacts contain an unclosed source-backed SDMMC0 register or fabric delta suitable for the next patch?
findings: The fixed register schema already captured the decisive delta: vendor IDMAC advances CHDA/CBDA during 4 KiB reads, while mainline reaches IDST=0x4000 with CHDA=DLBA and CBDA=0.

Existing artifacts already record that preserving vendor SAMP_DL, descriptor size/shift/DLBA, ER/no-ER, 64-bit DMA mask, streaming descriptors, store/MBUS/MSI-lite, NSI 0x580/0x584, threshold/CSDC, GCTRL access mode, and completion cleanup did not consume descriptor word 0.
Searches over UART logs and H-result files did not reveal an unclosed named SDMMC0 firewall/security/master-permission register or source-backed bit.
The remaining actionable evidence class is still external: maintainer/list/vendor reply or a newly found BOOT0/BL31/security/firewall source.
decision: No kernel patch. Existing register artifacts are consistent and already folded into the blocker; no unclosed local register delta justifies another behavior test.
next_responsible_step: Keep monitoring for public/list/vendor response or new source-backed SDMMC0 master/firewall/security evidence; avoid repeating timing/descriptor/fabric variants.
